schools.summary: Gill: this was after the plague related in the preceding chapter, perhaps before the war with Midian.
Keil & Delitzsch: the Israelites were to be mustered as the army of Jehovah by a fresh numbering before taking vengeance on Midian.

themes: after the plague, that the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Eleazar
a fresh muster of the army of the LORD
the old order passing into the new

schools.summary: Gill: the Levites were excepted; the count was to show that all the old generation that came out of Egypt were now dead, save two, as threatened, and that the land might be divided to them.
Keil & Delitzsch: take the sum from twenty years old and upward, all able to go to war.

themes: Take the sum of all the congregation... from twenty years old and upward... able to go to war
proving the old generation dead
preparing to divide the land

schools.summary: Gill: they spake with the heads of the people to assist in numbering; the princes who had helped thirty-eight years before were all dead, and another race succeeded, in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.

themes: Moses and Eleazar... spake with them in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho
a new race of princes, the old dead
Israel at the threshold of the land

schools.summary: Gill: at the same age as the sum taken thirty-eight years before, so that none could be above sixty of all who went into Canaan, except Joshua and Caleb, and some few in Levi.
Keil & Delitzsch: take the sum from twenty years old and upward, as the LORD commanded.

themes: Take the sum of the people, from twenty years old and upward
the same age-rule, a wholly new people
none of the first muster left save Joshua and Caleb

schools.summary: Gill: Reuben is taken first as Israel's eldest; four families descended from him, the men fit for war 43,730, a decrease of 2,770 since the last numbering, in part from the families of Dathan and Abiram cut off in the rebellion.

themes: Reuben, the eldest son of Israel: the children of Reuben
43,730, decreased by 2,770
sin thinning the ranks, the rebellion of Dathan and Abiram

schools.summary: Gill and Keil & Delitzsch: the families of Reuben (Hezron and Carmi), numbered 43,730 in all; the Hezronites and Carmites complete the four families of Reuben.

themes: the families of Reuben (Hezron and Carmi)... that were numbered of them
43,730 in all
the hand of God in the increase and decrease

schools.summary: Ellicott: a decrease of 2,770 compared with the former census (Num. 1:21), Dathan and Abiram having probably enlisted many of their tribe in their rebellion.
Wesley: the 'families' are the chief houses, subdivided into lesser branches.

themes: the Reubenites... were forty and three thousand and seven hundred and thirty
decreased by 2,770
the rebellion of Dathan and Abiram weakening the tribe

schools.summary: Gill: Pallu had but one son, Eliab, the father of Dathan and Abiram. Calvin: the curtailment of the superior line is introduced either to exalt God's grace, that Reuben was still numerous despite so great a loss, or to mark the cause of its diminution.

themes: the sons of Pallu; Eliab
the line where the rebellion arose
grace and justice both legible in the census

schools.summary: Gill: Nemuel either died without children or perished with his brethren in the conspiracy; the text recalls that Dathan and Abiram, famous in the congregation, strove against Moses and Aaron in the company of Korah, when they strove against the LORD.

themes: This is that Dathan and Abiram... who strove against Moses and against Aaron in the company of Korah
their revolt aimed against the LORD
the census a monument of warning

schools.summary: Gill: the earth swallowed up Dathan and Abiram, their wives, children, houses, and goods, together with Korah's company, while the fire devoured the 250; they 'became a sign,' a warning to others.

themes: the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed them up... and they became a sign
the judgment recalled in the census
God turning his judgments into a perpetual warning

schools.summary: Gill: they died not by pestilence, fire, or the swallowing earth, not being in their father's counsel, but following the doctrine of Moses (as the Targum says); they either disliked the scheme or repented and departed from it.

themes: Notwithstanding the children of Korah died not
grace sparing the children who would not follow the rebel
their seed later singers of the LORD (Ps. 42)

schools.summary: Gill: Simeon was numbered next, being under the standard of Reuben; two of his sons are named a little differently than in Gen. 46:10, and Ohad is omitted, perhaps dying without children.

themes: The sons of Simeon after their families
a son omitted, names altered (Gen. 46:10)
providence ordering the rise and fall of the houses

schools.summary: Gill and Keil & Delitzsch: the families of Simeon (Zerah and Shaul), numbered 22,200 in all; the family of the Zarhites and the Shaulites complete the tribe of Simeon.

themes: the families of Simeon (Zerah and Shaul)... that were numbered of them
22,200 in all
the hand of God in the increase and decrease

schools.summary: Ellicott: a decrease of 37,100 in Simeon; Zimri, the chief offender at Baal-peor, belonged to this tribe and probably led many astray with him; this is the only tribe on which Moses pronounced no blessing (Deut. 33).

themes: the families of the Simeonites, twenty and two thousand and two hundred
a decrease of 37,100, the heaviest fall
traced to Baal-peor and Zimri of Simeon (Deut. 33)

schools.summary: Gill: Gad was numbered next to Simeon, under the standard of Reuben; some names vary from Gen. 46:16; seven families sprang from Gad, now 40,500, diminished since the first muster.

themes: The children of Gad after their families
seven families, 40,500, diminished
the south-side tribes declining together

schools.summary: Keil & Delitzsch: the families of Ozni and Eri among the seven of Gad; Ozni answers to Ezbon of the earlier list (Gen. 46:16).

themes: Of Ozni, the family of the Oznites: of Eri, the family of the Erites
Ozni answering to Ezbon (Gen. 46:16)
every house of Gad reckoned

schools.summary: Ellicott: a decrease of 5,150; Reuben, Simeon, and Gad encamped together on the south of the tabernacle and had probably been mutually contaminated by each other's evil example.

themes: the children of Gad... forty thousand and five hundred
decreased by 5,150
the southern camp mutually contaminated

schools.summary: Gill: Judah had also Shelah, Pharez, and Zerah, from whom families sprang, but none from Er and Onan. Calvin: of the three sons left to Judah the tribe grew so numerous, magnifying God's grace.
Ellicott: Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan (Gen. 38:7-10).

themes: The sons of Judah were Er and Onan: and Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan
a great tribe from a reduced stock
God's grace, not nature's strength, the increase of his people

schools.summary: Keil & Delitzsch: the families of Judah by Shelah, Pharez, and Zerah, with the sons of Pharez, Hezron and Hamul; Judah numbered 76,500, the largest tribe.

themes: the sons of Judah... of Shelah... of Pharez... of Zerah
the largest tribe, the line of promise
the tribe of the coming King (Gen. 49:10)

schools.summary: Ellicott: Judah had five sons, but inasmuch as Er and Onan died childless, Hezron and Hamul were substituted in their place (Gen. 46:12).

themes: the sons of Pharez were; of Hezron... of Hamul
substituted for the childless Er and Onan (Gen. 46:12)
Hezron in the line of David and Christ (Matt. 1:3)

schools.summary: Wesley: the families of Judah were about two thousand more than they were (Num. 1:27), whereas the foregoing tribes were all diminished.

themes: the families of Judah... threescore and sixteen thousand and five hundred
increased while the others diminished
God's favour on Judah, the tribe of the King

schools.summary: Gill: Issachar was numbered next to Judah, under his standard; two sons named a little differently (Pua for Phuvah, Jashub for Job); four families, the warlike men 64,300, increased by 9,900 since the last muster.

themes: Of the sons of Issachar after their families
four families, 64,300, increased by 9,900
the blessing of God on the tribe of Judah's standard

schools.summary: Keil & Delitzsch: the families of Jashub and Shimron complete the four of Issachar, numbered 64,300; every branch named, the increase the gift of God.

themes: Of Jashub, the family of the Jashubites: of Shimron, the family of the Shimronites
the four families of Issachar completed
the increased tribe registered house by house

schools.summary: Gill: Zebulun was numbered next to Issachar, under the standard of Judah; three families, numbering 60,500, increased by 3,100.

themes: Of the sons of Zebulun after their families
three families, 60,500, increased by 3,100
the camp of Judah prospering together

schools.summary: Keil & Delitzsch: the Zebulunites at 60,500; the sons of Joseph, Manasseh and Ephraim, classified in two leading families, raised into founders of tribes (Gen. 48:5).

themes: the families of the Zebulunites... threescore thousand and five hundred
the turn to the sons of Joseph
Joseph's double portion in Manasseh and Ephraim (Gen. 48:5)

schools.summary: Gill: Manasseh is mentioned first, though Ephraim was preferred by Jacob and had the standard, not because he was firstborn but because he had now the greater increase, having one son Machir and a grandson Gilead, whose family was large.

themes: The sons of Joseph after their families were Manasseh and Ephraim
Manasseh first for his greater present increase
Jacob's word that the younger be greater (Gen. 48:19)

schools.summary: Keil & Delitzsch: eight families descended from Manasseh — one from Machir, one from Gilead, and six from the sons of Gilead — the genealogies of Num. 27, 36, and Joshua 17 harmonizing with this.

themes: Of the sons of Manasseh: of Machir... and Machir begat Gilead
eight families of Manasseh
the line leading toward Zelophehad and inheritance (Num. 27)

schools.summary: Keil & Delitzsch: the sons of Gilead — Jeezer, Helek, and the rest — the six families that with Machir and Gilead make up the eight of Manasseh; among them Hepher, whose son Zelophehad had only daughters.

themes: These are the sons of Gilead: of Jeezer... of Helek
the six families completing Manasseh
the line of Hepher and Zelophehad's daughters

schools.summary: Wesley: they were but 32,200 in the first census (Num. 1:35), now increased above twenty thousand, according to the prophecy of the fruitful bough (Gen. 49:22).

themes: the families of Manasseh... fifty and two thousand and seven hundred
increased above twenty thousand
the fruitful bough of Joseph (Gen. 49:22)

schools.summary: Gill: Ephraim's families were but three — the Shuthalhite, Bachrite, and Tahanite, with the Eranite springing from the first; the whole 32,500, a decrease of 8,000.

themes: the sons of Ephraim after their families
32,500, a decrease of 8,000
moving opposite to Manasseh's rise

schools.summary: Keil and Delitzsch: the families of Shuthelah and Eran (Ephraim), numbered 32,500 in all; the family of the Eranites springs from Shuthelah, completing the houses of Ephraim.

themes: the family of the Shuthelah and Eran (Ephraim)... that were numbered of them
32,500 in all
the hand of God in the increase and decrease

schools.summary: Ellicott: a decrease of 8,000; Jacob foretold that Ephraim should be greater than Manasseh (Gen. 48:19), and at the former census Ephraim was the larger and a standard-bearer, but at this census the Manassites exceeded the Ephraimites.

themes: the sons of Ephraim... thirty and two thousand and five hundred
Manasseh now exceeding Ephraim
a reversal only for a season, Jacob's word sure (Gen. 48:19)

schools.summary: Gill: Benjamin was numbered next to Ephraim, under his standard; five of his sons are omitted, perhaps dying without issue, the names varying a little (Ahiram for Ehi, Shupham and Hupham for Huppim and Muppim).

themes: The sons of Benjamin after their families
five sons' lines failing, names varied
providence ordering which houses continued

schools.summary: Keil and Delitzsch: the families of Shupham and Hupham (Benjamin), numbered 45,600 in all; the families of Shupham and Hupham among the houses of Benjamin, the tribe numbering forty-five thousand six hundred.

themes: the family of the Shupham and Hupham (Benjamin)... that were numbered of them
45,600 in all
the hand of God in the increase and decrease

schools.summary: Gill: Dan had but one son, Shuham (Hushim by transposition), yet from him the one family numbered 64,400, an increase of 1,700; the plural 'sons' is put for the one son or his posterity.

themes: the sons of Dan after their families: of Shuham
one family yet 64,400, the second largest
God multiplying a host from one root

schools.summary: Keil and Delitzsch: the families of Shuham (Dan), numbered 64,400 in all; the Shuhamites, the one family of Dan, numbered threescore and four thousand and four hundred, increased by 1,700.

themes: the family of the Shuham (Dan)... that were numbered of them
64,400 in all
the hand of God in the increase and decrease

schools.summary: Gill: Asher was numbered next to Dan, under his standard; one son is omitted, very probably dying childless; from the rest sprang the families named; Asher had a daughter, Serah, particularly mentioned.

themes: Of the children of Asher after their families: of Jimna... of Jesui... of Beriah
Serah his daughter remembered by name
none of God's people forgotten in his account

schools.summary: Keil and Delitzsch: the families of Heber and Malchiel (Asher), numbered 53,400 in all; the families of Heber and Malchiel from Beriah, with Sarah the daughter of Asher, the tribe numbering fifty-three thousand four hundred.

themes: the family of the Heber and Malchiel (Asher)... that were numbered of them
53,400 in all
the hand of God in the increase and decrease

schools.summary: Gill: Naphtali, the last, was numbered next to Asher, under the standard of Dan; four families — the Jahzeelite, Gunite, Jezerite, and Shillemite — numbering 45,400, less by 8,000 than when first numbered.

themes: Of the sons of Naphtali after their families: of Jahzeel
four families, 45,400, decreased by 8,000
closing the muster among the diminished tribes

schools.summary: Keil and Delitzsch: the families of Jezer and Shillem (Naphtali), numbered 45,400 in all; the families of Jezer and Shillem complete Naphtali, the tribe numbering forty-five thousand four hundred.

themes: the family of the Jezer and Shillem (Naphtali)... that were numbered of them
45,400 in all
the hand of God in the increase and decrease

schools.summary: Gill: the sum total of the twelve tribes, of persons twenty years old and upward, fit for war — 601,730.
Clarke: gives a comparative statement showing how much some tribes had decreased and others increased.

themes: the numbered of the children of Israel, six hundred thousand and a thousand seven hundred and thirty
the new generation nearly as many as the old
God's preserving hand keeping the whole

schools.summary: Gill: this was spoken after the sum of the people had been taken.
Keil & Delitzsch: the instructions concerning the distribution of the land.

themes: the LORD spake unto Moses
the census the basis of the inheritance
God counting his own and providing each a portion

schools.summary: Gill: it was to those who were numbered, and to none else.
Keil & Delitzsch: the division according to the number of names.

themes: Unto these the land shall be divided for an inheritance according to the number of names
to the numbered, each portion by its number
certain and fair in the parting of the land

schools.summary: Gill: a more numerous tribe was to have a greater part of the land, a smaller tribe a lesser share; the direction is given to Moses but not for him personally, for he never entered the land.

themes: To many thou shalt give the more inheritance, and to few... the less
each according to its number
the equity of God's law in dividing the land

schools.summary: Gill: the division was by lot, that it might appear to be according to the will of God, not left to the judgment of the chief magistrate, though bound by the rule of proportion.
Clarke: discusses the word goral for 'lot,' the stone or pebble used in casting it.

themes: the land shall be divided by lot... according to the names of the tribes
the allotment God's own choice
equity and sovereignty wed, the lot of the LORD (Prov. 16:33)

schools.summary: Gill: it was by the determination of the lot that the land was divided by inheritance, and that was of the Lord.
Wesley: the share that fell by lot to each tribe was distributed to its families and persons in such proportions as their numbers required.

themes: According to the lot shall the possession thereof be divided between many and few
the place by lot, the size by number
divine choice and equity together

schools.summary: Gill: the Levites were numbered not with the rest of the tribes but by themselves, as at the first; the three principal families were the Gershonite, Kohathite, and Merarite, from the three sons of Levi.

themes: these are they that were numbered of the Levites after their families
numbered apart by the three families of Levi
set apart to the sanctuary, not the sword

schools.summary: Keil & Delitzsch: the Levite families — Libnites, Hebronites, Mahlites, Mushites, Korathites — and Kohath begat Amram, the line of Moses and Aaron.

themes: the families of the Levites... the Korathites. And Kohath begat Amram
the line of the leaders and priests
Moses, Aaron, and Miriam from the house of Kohath

schools.summary: Ellicott: 'the daughter of Levi, whom her mother bare to Levi' — or who was born to Levi; some have supposed Jochebed a more remote descendant, but the plain sense is defended; she bare to Amram Aaron, Moses, and Miriam.

themes: Jochebed, the daughter of Levi... she bare unto Amram Aaron and Moses, and Miriam
the mother of Israel's deliverers
the woman of faith honoured in the record (Heb. 11:23)

schools.summary: Keil & Delitzsch: Nadab and Abihu died when they offered strange fire before the LORD; the Levites were not mustered with the rest because no inheritance was given them among Israel.

themes: And Nadab and Abihu died, when they offered strange fire before the LORD
the judgment recalled in the priestly line
even the priest's sons not spared (Lev. 10:1-2)

schools.summary: Ellicott: the number 23,000, against 22,000 or 22,300 at the former census (Num. 3:39); numbered from a month old, with no inheritance among Israel, for the LORD was their portion.

themes: those that were numbered of them were twenty and three thousand... no inheritance given them
counted from a month old as the LORD's
the LORD himself their portion (Num. 18:20)

schools.summary: Gill: these were numbered by Moses and Eleazar, who may have been concerned only in numbering the Levites, of which tribe they were.
Keil & Delitzsch: the numbering in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.

themes: numbered by Moses and Eleazar... in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho
the people at the threshold of the land
the wandering ended, the inheritance in sight

schools.summary: Gill: this respects the numbering of the Israelites, not the Levites, for some of Levi numbered then were still living, as Eleazar; of the lay tribes not a man of the first census survived.

themes: there was not a man of them whom Moses and Aaron... numbered... in the wilderness of Sinai
the whole first generation dead
the threatening fulfilled, none entering for unbelief (Heb. 3:19)

schools.summary: Gill: this was threatened of them and now fulfilled; there was not left a man save Caleb and Joshua, the only two of the spies who brought a good report; all the rest, and those who murmured at their ill report, were now dead.

themes: the LORD had said... They shall surely die in the wilderness... save Caleb... and Joshua
the threat fulfilled, the promise kept
God true in judgment and in mercy
